LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO. MOTION FOR SUMMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT RULES ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS FOLLOWS: AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, DENIED TO ALL RESPECTS; EXCEPT IT IS GRANTED AS TO THE TERMINATION ISSUES; DEMOTION ISSUES ARE DENIED, EXCEPT FOR THE HARASSMENT ISSUES, #10, & 11, WHICH ARE GRANTED; THE COURT DOES NOT RULE UPON THE LEAVE ISSUE, (TOO THIN AN ISSUE.). AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT'S RULING ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ALSO APPLIES TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT CITES MATHIEU VS. NORRELL CORP. (2004) 115 CAL.APP. 4TH 1174 AS GOOD LAW. PLAINTIFF RAISES TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE CLAIM BASED ON TIMING OF NOTICE RE: PLAINTIFF'S ADD & DEMOTION; THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED, PLAINTIFF RAISES TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE CLAIM BASED ON TIMING OF NOTICE RE: PLAINTIFF'S ADD & DEMOTION; SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, IS GRANTED, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING NEXUS BETWEEN DISABILITY AND HIS JUNE 2008 TERMINATION; FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FAIL ON A MATTER OF LAW; EXCLUSIVE WORKERS COMPENSATION REMEDY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; COURT REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO. MOTION FOR SUMMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Andrew D. Morrison, Esq. of Ross & Morrison (310) 285-0391 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Madelyn Jordan-Davis, Esq. of Foster Employment Law (510) 763-1900 Appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: The Court rules on Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication as follows: as to First cause of action, denied to all respects; except it is granted as to the termination issues; Demotion issues are denied, except for the harassment issues, #10, & 11, which are granted; the Court does not rule upon the leave issue, (too thin an issue.). As to the sixth cause of action, the court's ruling on the first cause of action also applies to the sixth cause of action. The Court cites Mathieu vs. Norrell Corp. (2004) 115 Cal.App. 4th 1174 as good law. Plaintiff raises triable issue of fact regarding failure to accommodate claim based on timing of notice re: plaintiff's ADD & demotion; third cause of action is denied, Plaintiff raises triable issue of fact regarding failure to accommodate claim based on timing of notice re: plaintiff's ADD & demotion; second cause of action, is granted, plaintiff fails to create triable issue of fact regarding nexus between disability and his June 2008 termination; fourth and fifth causes of action are granted. Plaintiff's claims fail on a matter of law; exclusive workers compensation remedy. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/AJR)Judge: A. James Robertson; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620
